actionLibraryList.title: Kneeling Thoracic Rotation
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Kneel on one knee, with the other leg in front, and the knee at a 90-degree angle.
2.Keep the chest up, maintain a neutral spine, and engage the core. Rotate the torso towards the lead leg, using the hands to assist the rotation.
3.Keep the hips stable and neutral throughout the movement.
4.After 3-4 repetitions, switch legs and repeat on the opposite side.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749521236285.jpg","context":"1.Kneel on one knee, with the other leg in front, and the knee at a 90-degree angle.\n2.Keep the hips neutral and stable, with the core engaged.\n3.Rotate the torso towards the lead leg, using the hands to actively assist the rotation."}]
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Engage your core throughout the exercise.

actionLibraryList.title: Cable Side Lunge
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and engage your core.
2.Attach the band to the working leg's ankle, with the leg pushing against the resistance.
3.Step the working leg out to the side and bend the knee into a lateral lunge.
4.Keep the other leg straight and lean your upper body slightly toward the working leg.
5.Drive through the working leg and glutes to return to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750407767763.jpg","context":"1.Working leg pushes against the band."},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750407772432.jpg","context":"1.Step the working leg out to the side and bend the knee into a lateral lunge.\n2.Keep the other leg straight with the knee facing forward."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Inhale as you squat down, exhale as you stand up.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Strong activation in the glutes and legs.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Keep the knee from collapsing inward.

actionLibraryList.title: Standing Dual-Handle Shrug
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and engage your core.
2.Hold the handles with palms facing each other.
3.Let your arms hang naturally and shrug your shoulders up toward your ears.
4.Keep your arms and elbows straight, using only your shoulders to lift.
5.Pause briefly at the top, then slowly return to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice1Txt: Lock the pulleys to the floor, with the handles attached.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: Shrug your shoulders vertically, aiming to touch your ears with them.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Avoid holding your breath and maintain even breathing.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"context":"1.Hold the handles with palms facing each other.\n2.Keep your elbows naturally straight and relaxed.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749435448839.jpg"},{"context":"1.Shrug your shoulders up toward your ears.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749435455390.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Breathe steadily, avoiding holding your breath.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Shrug your shoulders vertically, lifting them as close to your ears as possible.

actionLibraryList.title: Pallof Press to Woodchops
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Position the handle at chest height and stand sideways, holding the handle with both hands in front of your chest.
2.Press the handle forward until your arms are fully extended, keeping it stable, chest facing forward, and core engaged.
3.Rotate your hands and upper body to the side while keeping your hips facing forward.
4.Pause 1–2 seconds to feel the anti-rotation engagement, then slowly return to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice1Txt: Adjust the grip to a mid-high position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Stand with your body facing sideways.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: Bring the cable to your chest, extend it out, then rotate it to the side.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: Keep your hips square, and do not let them rotate.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Exhale as you press forward and rotate.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: Stabilize your trunk by engaging your core.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"context":"1.Stand facing sideways, gripping the handles with both hands against your chest.\n2.Make sure to keep your hips square and engage your core to stabilize the movement.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749433573500.jpg"},{"context":"1.Slowly push the handles forward, then rotate to the side.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749433579767.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale as you press forward and rotate..
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Stabilize your trunk by engaging your core.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Keep your hips facing forward; avoid letting them rotate with the pull.

actionLibraryList.title: Seated Dual-Handle Underhand Row
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Adjust the grips to abdominal height or slightly higher.
2.Pull the handles until your elbows slightly pass your body, then slowly return.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice1Txt: Set the cable to position 6 or 7
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Sitting on the bench, keep your back straight, grab the handles with an overhand grip, arms extended, and engage your core
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: Pull the handles until your elbows slightly pass your body, then slowly return
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: Keep your forearms aligned with the cable
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Exhale while exerting force, inhale while returning
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749007433468.jpg","context":"1.Underhand grip."},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749007437381.jpg","context":"1.Keep your head up and chest lifted.\n2.Keep your elbows tucked in.\n3.Squeeze your shoulder blades."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale while exerting force, inhale while returning.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Keep your forearms aligned with the cable.

actionLibraryList.title: Jumping Lunges To Squat
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Start in a standing position with feet hip-width apart. Squat down to a deep squat and then explosively jump into the air.
2.Land softly and immediately jump again, landing in a lunge position.
3.Once more, jump and land back in a squat position, then jump and land into the opposite lunge position.
4.Ensure you’re familiar with the proper form for lunges and squats before attempting this movement.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Start with a squat with arms throw out to the front.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: Jump and come into a lunge with your left leg.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: Keep a flat and natural spine. The leg should bounce back quickly to finish the alternate motion.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Inhale in squat and exhale in jumping lunge.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: Brace your core and recruit your glutes.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"context":"1.Keep your back straight, engage your core.\n2.Squat down to a deep squat position.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750737168335.jpg"},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750737172809.jpg","context":"1.Keep your back straight.\n2.After the squat, alternate into lunges."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale as you exert force, inhale as you return.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Maintain an upright posture, and ensure the legs rebound quickly when switching movements.
